description: Sprout Learning Curriculum — Toddler-to-Young-Adult AI growth roadmap
critical_rule: Never skip stages. Sequential progression is mandatory. Skipping causes confused-but-confident behavior.

stages.name: Toddler Brain
capabilities: 2-3 word combinations: 'dog run', 'big car', 'I eat'
Basic object recognition and labeling
Simple action-object pairing

tests: Can label common objects correctly
Can pair actions with objects
Can use basic descriptors

stages.name: Child Language
capabilities: Full simple sentences: 'The dog runs fast'
Pronoun usage: 'I like this', 'She is happy'
Basic tense awareness: present, past, future

tests: Can form grammatically correct simple sentences
Uses pronouns correctly in context
Demonstrates basic tense understanding
Can answer simple who/what/where questions

stages.name: Pre-Teen Logic
capabilities: Complex sentences: 'I think this is good because it works'
Understanding ambiguity and context
Basic reasoning and explanation
Comparison and contrast

teach: Basic logical reasoning (if X then Y)

tests: Can explain why something happens
Can handle words with multiple meanings
Can make comparisons between concepts
Can follow basic logical chains

stages.name: Teen Intelligence
capabilities: Opinion forming and expression
Structured explanations and arguments
Style variation based on context
Intent detection in user messages
Domain-specific vocabulary (tech, social, science)

tests: Can form and defend opinions with evidence
Can adapt tone to different contexts
Can detect user intent beyond literal meaning
Can produce structured, coherent responses
Can handle domain-specific conversations

stages.name: Young Adult Intelligence
capabilities: Critical analysis — questioning assumptions, evaluating sources, spotting logical fallacies
Emotional intelligence — reading between the lines, empathetic responses, tactful delivery
Independent reasoning — forming original perspectives beyond training data
Creative problem-solving — lateral thinking, novel approaches, connecting disparate ideas
Self-aware communication — knowing what it knows and what it doesn't, honest about uncertainty
Nuanced worldview — understanding that most issues have multiple valid perspectives
Contextual code-switching — adjusting depth, formality, and complexity to match the user
Long-form coherence — maintaining consistent reasoning across extended conversations
Metacognition — reflecting on its own thought process and reasoning quality

patterns: multi-perspective analysis (consider X, but also Y, and even Z)
self-correcting reasoning (wait, actually... let me reconsider)
empathetic framing (I get why you'd think that — here's another angle)
structured argumentation with concessions (while X is true, Y matters more because...)
creative analogies and metaphors to explain complex ideas
conversational depth scaling (simple when simple works, deep when depth is needed)
teach: Independent thought formation beyond pattern matching
Contextual communication — matching tone and depth to the situation
Long-form reasoning coherence across multi-turn conversations
Handling ambiguity and incomplete information gracefully

tests: Can critically analyze claims and identify weak reasoning
Can respond with emotional intelligence and appropriate empathy
Can form original, well-reasoned perspectives on novel topics
Can solve problems creatively using lateral thinking
Can honestly assess its own knowledge boundaries
Can present multiple perspectives on complex issues fairly
Can adapt communication style fluidly based on context
Can maintain coherent reasoning across 20+ conversation turns
Can reflect on and improve its own reasoning process

bilingual_extension.description: French language module can be activated at any stage
bilingual_extension.activation_rule: Only activate after Stage 1 English graduation
bilingual_extension.approach: Cross-reference French words with known English equivalents
